observation_summary: The application throws a JsonMappingException with 'Operation not supported' when attempting to serialize binary data using an InputStream, because the ToXmlGenerator class lacks an implementation for the writeBinary(Base64Variant, InputStream, int) method.
hypothesis: The ToXmlGenerator class is missing a required method implementation for handling binary data streams, which is expected by the Jackson serialization framework. The current implementation only supports byte arrays, causing an unsupported operation error when an InputStream is provided.
prediction: Implementing the missing writeBinary method in ToXmlGenerator, along with helper methods to read the InputStream into a buffer or stream it, will resolve the JsonMappingException.
experiment_rationale: The fix diff shows the addition of a complete method implementation (writeBinary) and supporting private methods (writeStreamAsBinary, toFullBuffer) that were previously absent. This confirms that the functionality was missing from the class design.
reasoning_summary: The bug is classified as Function/Class/Object because the fix involves adding an entirely missing capability (the writeBinary method for InputStream) to the ToXmlGenerator class. This is a design-level omission rather than a local algorithmic error or a simple guard/value correction.
evidence_used: The bug report explicitly states: 'ToXmlGenerator does not implement this method.'
The fix diff shows the addition of the entire writeBinary method and its helper methods.

alternative_types.why_not_primary: While the fix involves writing a method, the primary issue is the absence of the required interface capability, not an error in an existing algorithm.
